_comment: Model profiles - scratch's internal policy for how to use Venice models. Sourced by lib/model.sh (model:profile:* functions). Tracked in the repo, not user-configurable. Updates are code changes. Validated against the Venice model registry via model:profile:validate.

smart._comment: Maximum reasoning. Use when correctness matters more than latency.

balanced._comment: Same model as smart but with reasoning disabled. The default text profile for general work. Avoid reasoning_effort=minimal: OpenAI added that value for a brief window, very few clients used it, and downstream providers (including Venice for some models like zai-org-glm-5-1) return a 500 'Inference processing failed' when it arrives. Use 'none' instead.

fast._comment: Reasoning disabled. Use for high-volume or latency-sensitive operations where some quality loss is acceptable.

long-context._comment: 1M token context window. The default profile for the accumulator and any other feature that processes oversized inputs. chars_per_token is the per-profile float used by token-budgeting code (lib/accumulator.sh) to estimate request sizes; see data/models.md for the full schema.

coding._comment: Code generation and analysis. Extends smart for max reasoning, lowers temperature for determinism, disables Venice's default system preamble so strict instructions are not diluted.

web._comment: Web-augmented research. Extends balanced and turns on Venice's web search with citations.
